seoTitle: Manually Add Credit Card Transactions to QuickBooks
metaDescription: Log an individual credit card charge in QuickBooks Online by hand when it's missing from your bank feed or needs immediate entry.
intro: Not every transaction shows up right away on your bank feed, and sometimes you'd rather log a charge at the point of sale. This guide shows how to manually enter an individual credit card transaction into QuickBooks Online.

steps.heading: Open a new expense
steps.body: Click + New, then select Expense from the Vendors column.

steps.heading: Select the payee
steps.body: Choose the merchant profile from your live directory.

steps.heading: Choose the payment account
steps.body: Set Payment Account to the correct credit card liability account and select Credit Card as the payment method.

steps.heading: Categorize and enter the amount
steps.body: Select the correct expense category and key in the transaction amount.

steps.heading: Attach a receipt and save
steps.body: Drag a digital receipt into the attachment area, add a memo for the project or purpose, then click Save and Close.

faq.question: Why manually enter a card transaction instead of waiting for the feed?
faq.answer: Manual entry keeps your ledger up to date immediately, which is useful if you want real-time tracking or if the charge hasn't appeared on your feed yet.

faq.question: What happens if this transaction later appears in my bank feed?
faq.answer: You'll want to match the incoming feed line to your manual entry instead of adding it as a new transaction to avoid duplicates.

faq.question: Can I attach a receipt to a manual entry?
faq.answer: Yes, you can drag and drop a scanned receipt or PDF directly into the attachment workspace on the expense form.

faq.question: Do I need to select the exact card used?
faq.answer: Yes, choosing the correct credit card liability account keeps your card balances accurate.
